recipes.title: 96 Calorie Protein Cakey Frosted Cosmic Brownie
recipes.description: A high-protein, low-calorie cakey brownie topped with a creamy chocolate frosting and optional confetti chips. Each brownie is under 100 calories and packed with protein thanks to pumpkin, egg whites, and protein powder.
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a mixing bowl, combine the wet ingredients: almond milk, egg whites, and canned pumpkin. Mix well.
In a separate mixing bowl, combine the dry ingredients: all-purpose flour, granulated sugar substitute, protein powder, cocoa powder, baking soda, and baking powder. Mix well.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix thoroughly until fully combined, making sure to avoid any clumping. Mix again to ensure a smooth batter.
Spray a large square or rectangular baking dish with cooking spray, or line it with parchment paper.
Pour the brownie batter into the prepared baking dish and spread evenly.
Bake at 350°F for 35 minutes. Remove slightly underdone as they will continue to cook out of the oven.
While the brownies are baking, combine the frosting ingredients in a mixing bowl: Greek yogurt, confectioners sugar substitute, and cocoa powder. Mix until smooth, then refrigerate for 10 minutes.
Once brownies are done and have cooled slightly, spread the chilled frosting evenly over the top. Optionally, sprinkle with confetti or candy chips.
For a firmer frosting, place the frosted brownies in the fridge for an additional 10+ minutes.
Slice into a 3x3 grid to yield 9 brownies. Serve and enjoy!

recipes.title: Crispy Garlic Parmesan Chicken Wraps
recipes.description: Sheet pan crispy garlic parmesan chicken wraps with a creamy garlic parmesan sauce, fresh vegetables, and low-carb wraps. High-protein, low-effort, and perfect for meal prep or feeding a crowd.

ingredients.name: boneless skinless chicken thighs
ingredients.raw_text: 600g Raw Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs, cut into cubes

ingredients.name: chicken breast
ingredients.raw_text: 600g Raw Chicken Breast, cut into cubes

ingredients.name: grated parmesan
ingredients.notes: for chicken marinade; plus extra for finishing

steps: Slice chicken breast and thighs into cubes. Add salt, chilli flakes, parsley, Italian herbs, paprika, garlic granules, grated parmesan, and olive oil. Mix until all pieces are well coated.
Line a sheet pan with parchment paper and spread the marinated chicken evenly in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd. Spray a light layer of avocado cooking spray over the chicken.
Bake in a preheated oven at 200°C (390°F) for 20-22 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and crispy.
While the chicken is still hot, sprinkle over extra parmesan cheese and fresh parsley. Toss well until evenly coated and extra cheesy.
To make the creamy garlic parmesan sauce, melt light butter in a pan over low heat. Add Italian herbs, garlic powder, and paprika, and toast for 1 minute.
Pour in the light evaporated milk, then add the grated parmesan and light cream cheese. Stir continuously over low heat until the sauce is smooth and creamy. Remove from heat.

Assemble the wraps: lay out a low-carb wrap and layer with shredded lettuce, chopped tomatoes, chopped onions, a portion of crispy garlic parmesan chicken, and a drizzle of the creamy sauce. Serve immediately and enjoy.

For storage, keep the chicken and sauce in separate airtight containers in the fridge. To reheat, warm chicken in an oven or air fryer at 180°C (370°F) for 4-5 minutes, and warm sauce in a pan before assembling fresh wraps.

recipes.title: Loaded Egg Salad
recipes.description: A hearty and flavorful egg salad loaded with crispy bacon, sun-dried tomatoes, shallots, and fresh dill. Perfect on toast, as a dip, or eaten straight from the container.
steps: Place all 12 eggs into a large pot and cover with cold water. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat and cook for 10 minutes.
While eggs are boiling, cook the entire package of bacon in a skillet over medium heat until crispy. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain, then chop or crumble into pieces.
Once eggs are done, transfer them to an ice bath or run under cold water to cool. Peel all the eggs.
Add the peeled eggs to a large bowl and crack/roughly mash them with a fork or potato masher.
Add the crispy bacon, diced shallots, chopped sun-dried tomatoes, 2 large spoonfuls of mayonnaise, a spoonful of mustard, a squeeze of lemon juice, chopped fresh dill, and smoked paprika to the bowl.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Mash and mix everything together until well combined but still slightly chunky. Serve on toast, as a dip, or straight from the container.

recipes.title: Ultimate Breakfast Sandwich
recipes.description: A massive high-protein breakfast bagel sandwich loaded with top sirloin steak, cheesy scrambled eggs, crispy beef bacon, and a drizzle of honey. Make a batch of eight, freeze them, and reheat for an easy grab-and-go breakfast all week.
steps: Season the top sirloin with salt, pepper, and garlic powder on all sides.
Cook the steak in a pan over medium heat for a few minutes per side until it reaches an internal temperature of 125°F (52°C), then remove from the pan and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Dice the rested steak into bite-sized pieces and set aside.
In the same pan, cook the beef bacon strips until crisp, then remove and set aside, leaving the drippings in the pan.
Crack the eggs into the pan with the drippings and scramble or fry as preferred.
Top the eggs with the shredded low-fat cheddar cheese and allow it to melt completely.
Slice the bagels and toast them cut-side down in the same pan, soaking up the remaining juices, until golden brown.
Assemble each sandwich by layering diced steak, cheesy eggs, and crispy beef bacon onto the toasted bagel bottom, then finish with a drizzle of honey and cap with the bagel top.
To store, wrap each sandwich individually in foil and refrigerate for up to 4 days or freeze for longer storage.
To reheat from fridge: bake wrapped in foil at 350°F (175°C) for 10–12 minutes. To reheat from frozen: thaw overnight in the fridge, then follow fridge reheating instructions.

recipes.title: Two-Ingredient High Protein Egg Bites
recipes.description: Fluffy, high-protein egg bites made with just eggs and cottage cheese. A quick and easy homemade alternative to Starbucks egg bites that can be prepped in bulk and frozen for later.
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
Add 6 eggs, 1 cup of cottage cheese, and a pinch of salt to a blender.
Blend the mixture until smooth and fully combined.
Grease a 12-cup muffin tin generously with butter.
Pour the blended egg mixture evenly into each muffin cup, filling about 3/4 of the way.
Bake in the preheated oven for 22-25 minutes, or until the egg bites are set and lightly golden on top.
Allow to cool slightly, then pop them out of the muffin tin.
Serve warm, or let cool completely and store in the freezer. Reheat in the microwave or air fryer as needed.

recipes.title: Seared Ahi Tuna Steaks with Yuzu Soy Dressing
recipes.description: Quick and easy seared ahi tuna steaks coated in togarashi seasoning, served sliced thin over a yuzu soy dressing with green onions and sesame seeds. A high-protein meal that comes together in minutes.
steps: Make the soy dressing by combining yuzu juice, soy sauce, mirin, rice vinegar, and honey in a small bowl. Whisk together and set aside.
Thaw the ahi tuna steaks in a glass bowl if frozen. Pat dry with paper towels.
Coat the tuna steaks lightly with oil and generously season all sides with togarashi seasoning.
Heat a skillet or cast iron pan over high heat until very hot. Sear the tuna steaks for about 45 seconds per side, leaving the center rare.
Transfer the seared tuna to the refrigerator for about 3 minutes to stop the cooking process.
Remove from the fridge and slice the tuna very thin against the grain.
Spoon the yuzu soy dressing onto a serving plate, arrange the sliced tuna on top, and garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Tiramisu Bowl
recipes.description: A high-protein twist on classic tiramisu made with vanilla Greek yogurt, espresso, and ladyfinger cookies. Ready in minutes and best enjoyed after chilling overnight.
steps: Add nonfat vanilla Greek yogurt to a large mixing bowl.
Pour in the cooled brewed espresso and mix until smooth.
Add espresso powder, vanilla bean paste, and a pinch of salt, then stir until fully combined.
Add maple syrup and stir again until everything is well incorporated.
Break or layer in the ladyfinger cookies and gently fold into the mixture.
Sprinkle cocoa powder evenly over the top.
Refrigerate for a few hours or overnight before serving.

recipes.title: Keto Peanut Butter Mousse
recipes.description: A fluffy, creamy, and peanut buttery keto-friendly dessert with only 3 net carbs per serving. Quick and easy to make with just a few simple ingredients.
steps: In a large mixing bowl, beat the heavy whipping cream with an electric mixer until stiff peaks form. Set aside.
In a separate bowl, beat together the cream cheese and peanut butter until smooth and well combined.
Add the powdered erythritol (or preferred keto sweetener) and vanilla extract to the peanut butter mixture and mix until fully incorporated.
Gently fold the whipped cream into the peanut butter mixture in two or three additions, being careful not to deflate the cream.
Taste and adjust sweetness if needed. Spoon or pipe the mousse into serving cups.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving, or serve immediately. Optionally garnish with a drizzle of peanut butter or crushed peanuts.

recipes.title: High-Protein Cinnamon Rolls
recipes.description: Easy two-ingredient dough cinnamon rolls made with Greek yogurt and protein flour, delivering over 20 grams of protein per roll. Topped with a creamy vanilla protein icing.
steps: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a mixing bowl, combine 170g fat-free Greek yogurt, 132g protein flour, 1/2 tsp baking powder, and 1/2 tsp salt. Mix until a dough forms.
Lightly flour your work surface and knead the dough until smooth.
Flour your rolling pin and roll the dough out into a flat rectangle.
Slice the dough into even strips.
Lightly spray or brush each strip with cooking spray or a small amount of oil.
Generously sprinkle cinnamon over each strip.
Roll each strip tightly into a small cinnamon roll shape.
Place the rolls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake at 350°F for 20–25 minutes, until lightly golden.
While the rolls bake, prepare the icing by mixing 1.5 scoops of vanilla protein powder with 50–60ml of your milk of choice until smooth and drizzleable.
Remove rolls from the oven, let cool slightly, then drizzle the protein icing over the top and serve.

recipes.title: Protein Oreo Cheesecake Cups (Cottage Cheese)
recipes.description: A high-protein, no-bake cheesecake cup made with cottage cheese and Oreo cookies. Each serving delivers around 308 calories and 34g of protein.
steps: Add Oreo cookies to a food processor or zip-lock bag and crush into fine crumbs.
Mix crushed Oreo crumbs with a small amount of melted butter until combined, then press into the bottom of small cups or jars to form the crust layer.
In a blender or food processor, combine cottage cheese, cream cheese, vanilla extract, and sweetener of choice. Blend until completely smooth and creamy.
Fold in vanilla or cookies-and-cream flavored protein powder into the cheesecake mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Spoon or pipe the cheesecake filling on top of the Oreo crust in each cup.
Optionally, top each cup with crushed Oreo pieces or a whole mini Oreo for garnish.
Refrigerate for at least 1-2 hours until set, then serve chilled.

recipes.title: Spicy Tuna Crispy Rice
recipes.description: Golden, pan-fried sushi rice squares topped with a creamy, spicy tuna mixture and fresh avocado. A restaurant-style appetizer that's bold, satisfying, and visually stunning.

ingredients.name: scallions
ingredients.notes: thinly sliced, plus more for garnish

steps: Cook 3 cups of sushi rice according to package instructions. Once cooked, fold in ¼ cup of seasoned rice vinegar while the rice is still warm.
Line a baking tray or rectangular dish with plastic wrap. Spread and press the seasoned rice firmly and evenly into the tray to create a compact, uniform layer about 1 inch thick.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight (at least 8 hours) until the rice is firm and cold.
The next day, remove the rice from the tray and cut it into small, even rectangles or squares approximately 1.5 x 2.5 inches.
In a mixing bowl, combine the drained tuna, mayonnaise, sriracha, sesame oil, soy sauce, juice of half a lemon, and chopped scallions. Mix well until fully combined. Taste and adjust seasoning.

Heat a generous amount of vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Once the oil is hot and shimmering, fry the rice pieces in batches for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy. Do not overcrowd the pan.
Remove the crispy rice pieces and drain on a paper towel-lined plate.
Slice the avocado and season with lemon juice and salt to taste.
To assemble, place a slice of avocado on each crispy rice piece, then top with a spoonful of the spicy tuna mixture.
Garnish with sesame seeds and additional sliced scallions. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Overnight Chocolate Chip Protein Cookie Dough
recipes.description: A high-protein, edible cookie dough made with vanilla protein powder and Greek yogurt. Refrigerate overnight for a thick, scoopable treat that tastes indulgent but fits your macros.

ingredients.raw_text: 50g water, added slowly depending on your protein powder
ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: add slowly; adjust amount based on your protein powder's absorbency

steps: In a bowl, combine the vanilla protein powder, oat flour, baking soda, and salt. Mix the dry ingredients together until well combined.
Add the nonfat Greek yogurt, vanilla extract, and butter extract to the dry ingredients and stir to combine.

Slowly add water a little at a time, mixing as you go, until the batter comes together. The texture should resemble cake batter at this stage — adjust water amount based on your protein powder's absorbency.
Taste the mixture and add granulated stevia to your preferred sweetness level. Fold in the mini chocolate chips.
Cover the bowl and refrigerate for 3–4 hours (or overnight). The mixture will thicken into a scoopable, cookie dough-like consistency.
Serve cold, directly from the fridge, and enjoy by the bowlful.

recipes.title: Korean Beef Rice Bowls
recipes.description: A quick and flavorful Korean-inspired ground beef rice bowl with a savory-sweet soy garlic sauce, served over jasmine rice and topped with a runny fried egg and green onions. A comforting weeknight dinner that comes together in under 30 minutes.
steps: Cook jasmine rice by combining 1 cup jasmine rice with 1 cup water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until water is absorbed. Fluff with a fork and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together the low sodium soy sauce, sesame oil, finely chopped garlic, and brown sugar until the sugar is dissolved. Set the sauce aside.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef and season with salt and pepper. Break the beef down very fine as it cooks.

Once the beef is mostly cooked through but still slightly pink, pour in the sauce mixture and stir to combine. Continue cooking for 2-3 minutes until the sauce is absorbed and the beef is fully cooked and glossy.
In a separate small skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of canola oil over medium heat. Crack in the egg and fry until the whites are set but the yolk remains runny, about 2-3 minutes.
Divide the jasmine rice into bowls. Top with the Korean beef mixture, a fried egg, and sliced green onions. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: The OG Chicken Crust Pizza
recipes.description: A high-protein, low-calorie pizza made with a ground chicken crust, topped with classic pizza sauce and fat-free mozzarella. Under 500 calories and packed with 67g of protein.
steps: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a baking sheet or pizza pan with parchment paper.
In a bowl, combine the ground chicken, oat flour, and garlic salt. Mix until well combined.
Press the chicken mixture onto the prepared pan into a thin, round pizza crust shape, about 1/4 inch thick.
Bake the crust for 12–15 minutes, or until cooked through and lightly golden.
Remove from the oven and spread the pizza sauce evenly over the crust.
Sprinkle the fat-free mozzarella cheese on top.
Return to the oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Bacon, Sausage, and Egg Breakfast Wraps
recipes.description: Hearty make-ahead breakfast burritos loaded with bacon, sausage, and eggs — a budget-friendly copycat of a popular gas station staple. Make a big batch once a week and reheat in minutes.

ingredients.name: breakfast sausage
ingredients.notes: bulk or links removed from casing, cooked and crumbled

steps: Cook the bacon in a large skillet or oven at 400°F (200°C) until crispy. Drain on paper towels and chop or crumble into pieces.
Cook the breakfast sausage in a skillet over medium heat, breaking it into crumbles, until fully browned and cooked through. Drain excess grease.

Crack all eggs into a large bowl, season with salt and pepper, and whisk well. Scramble the eggs in a large buttered skillet over medium-low heat, stirring gently until just set and still slightly soft. Remove from heat.
Shred or slice the cheese if not pre-shredded.
Warm the large flour tortillas briefly in a dry skillet or microwave so they are pliable and easy to fold.
Lay a tortilla flat and layer on a portion of scrambled eggs, crumbled sausage, bacon pieces, and shredded cheese down the center.
Fold in the sides of the tortilla, then roll up tightly from the bottom to form a burrito. Wrap each completed burrito in parchment paper.
Repeat with remaining tortillas and filling ingredients to make all 16 wraps.
Place a few wraps in the refrigerator for the week and store the rest in the freezer in a zip-lock bag or airtight container for up to several months.
To reheat: microwave in the parchment paper for about 1 minute (from fridge) or 2–3 minutes (from frozen), which steams the wrap. Alternatively, unwrap and crisp in a hot skillet for best texture.

recipes.title: Spicy Alfredo Chicken Pasta Meal Prep
recipes.description: A high-protein meal prep featuring seasoned chicken breast and Banza pasta tossed in a creamy spicy cottage cheese Alfredo sauce. Makes 5 servings perfect for hitting your macros on a budget.
steps: Dice one red bell pepper into small pieces and set aside.
Season 2 lbs of chicken breast with 2 teaspoons each of salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, and Italian seasoning.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat and cook the seasoned chicken until golden brown and cooked through (internal temperature of 165°F). Set aside and slice or cube.
Cook both boxes of Banza chickpea pasta according to package directions. Drain and set aside.
To make the spicy Alfredo sauce, add 550g fat-free cottage cheese, the diced red bell pepper, 80g reduced-fat cream cheese, 10g hot sauce, and 1 cup of fat-free milk into a blender.
Blend the sauce mixture until completely smooth and creamy.
In a large pot or bowl, combine the cooked Banza pasta and sliced chicken.
Add the entire bag of Parmesan cheese and pour in the blended spicy Alfredo sauce. Toss everything together until well coated.
Divide evenly into 5 meal prep containers and refrigerate for up to 5 days.

recipes.title: High-Protein Banza Beef Pasta Bake
recipes.description: A hearty, macro-friendly pasta bake made with lean ground beef, Banza chickpea pasta, and a creamy blended cottage cheese tomato sauce. Perfect for meal prepping all week to maximize protein intake.

ingredients.name: Banza chickpea pasta
ingredients.notes: Any shape; cook according to package instructions

ingredients.name: fat-free shredded mozzarella cheese
ingredients.notes: Approximately 8 oz bag; used as topping

ingredients.name: fat-free milk
ingredients.notes: Blended into sauce to thin consistency

steps: Dice the sweet onion and green pepper into bite-sized pieces.
Heat a large skillet or pan over medium-high heat. Add the diced green pepper and onion and sauté for 3-4 minutes until softened.

Add 2 lbs of 93/7 ground beef to the pan with the vegetables. Season with 1 tablespoon of salt, black pepper, paprika, and Italian seasoning. Cook, breaking up the meat, until fully browned. Let the beef fat absorb into the peppers and onions for added flavor. Drain any excess fat if desired.
While the beef cooks, boil both boxes of Banza pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.

In a blender, combine the 12 oz marinara sauce, 3 cloves of garlic, 80g parmesan, 550g fat-free cottage cheese, and ½ cup fat-free milk. Blend until smooth and creamy to create the cheesy tomato sauce.
In a large pot or deep baking dish, combine the cooked pasta, the beef, pepper, and onion mixture, and pour in the blended cheesy tomato sauce. Stir to combine evenly.
Top with the entire bag of fat-free mozzarella cheese.
Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 15-20 minutes until the mozzarella is melted and bubbly, or heat through on the stovetop over medium-low heat until everything is warmed and the cheese is melted.
Divide into 5 equal servings and store in meal prep containers for the week.

recipes.title: High Protein Beef Bacon Pasta
recipes.description: A hearty, high-protein meal prep pasta featuring ground beef, beef bacon, peppers, onions, and a creamy blended cottage cheese sauce. Makes 10 meals at approximately 600 calories each.

ingredients.name: seasoning blend
ingredients.notes: use preferred seasonings for sautéing vegetables; go light as bacon adds significant flavor

steps: Chop 3 bell peppers and 2 large onions into bite-sized pieces.
Heat a large skillet over high heat and cook the chopped bell peppers and onions with your preferred seasonings until softened and slightly charred.
Add 3 pounds of 96/4 lean ground beef to the skillet with the peppers and onions. Break up and cook the beef thoroughly.
In a separate pan, cook 20 slices of beef bacon until crispy. Reserve the bacon grease.
Once the bacon is cooked, add the bacon and reserved grease back into the beef, peppers, and onions. Note: do not heavily season the meat mixture — the bacon provides most of the flavor.
Boil 8 servings (16 ounces) of your preferred protein pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.

To make the sauce, combine 880g low-fat cottage cheese, 30g honey, 1 bag fat-free mozzarella cheese, 50g 1/3-fat cream cheese, 50g Pecorino Romano, and 300ml fat-free Fairlife milk in a blender. Add pepper to taste and blend until completely smooth.
In a large pot, combine the cooked pasta, beef and bacon mixture, and the blended cheese sauce. Stir to combine.
Let the mixture simmer together for 10–15 minutes over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and everything is well incorporated.
Divide evenly into 10 meal prep containers (approximately 16.9–17.2 oz each). Refrigerate or freeze.
To reheat from refrigerator: microwave for 1 minute 30 seconds. To reheat from frozen: microwave 1 minute 30 seconds, stir, then microwave an additional 45 seconds.

recipes.title: Oat Recharge Smoothie
recipes.description: A creamy, comforting smoothie that blends the soluble fiber of oats with the anti-inflammatory benefits of cinnamon. Ideal for those with sensitive stomachs, it helps regulate digestion and keeps you feeling satisfied for longer.

ingredients.name: plain greek yoghurt
ingredients.raw_text: ½ cup plain Greek yoghurt (or dairy-free alternative)

ingredients.name: unsweetened almond milk
ingredients.raw_text: ½ cup unsweetened almond milk (or milk of choice)

ingredients.name: chia seeds
ingredients.raw_text: 1 tsp chia seeds (optional, for extra fibre)

ingredients.name: protein powder
ingredients.raw_text: 1 scoop unflavored or vanilla protein powder

steps: Add the rolled oats to the blender first to allow them to break down more easily.
Add the banana (fresh or frozen), plain Greek yoghurt, and unsweetened almond milk.
Add the ground cinnamon, chia seeds (if using), and protein powder.
Add a handful of ice.
Blend on high until smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Pineapple Power Smoothie
recipes.description: A gut-health smoothie that blends probiotic-rich yoghurt with the digestive benefits of pineapple to support gut health and alleviate feelings of bloating. Creamy, tangy, and packed with nutrients to help refuel and recover after a high-intensity workout.

ingredients.name: pineapple
ingredients.raw_text: ½ cup fresh or frozen pineapple

ingredients.name: plain greek yoghurt
ingredients.notes: or coconut yoghurt for a dairy-free option
ingredients.raw_text: ½ cup plain Greek yoghurt (or coconut yoghurt for a dairy-free option)

ingredients.name: unsweetened almond milk
ingredients.raw_text: ½ cup unsweetened almond milk (or milk of choice)

ingredients.name: flaxseeds
ingredients.raw_text: 1 tsp flaxseeds (for fibre and healthy fats)

steps: Add the fresh or frozen pineapple and frozen banana to a blender.
Add the plain Greek yoghurt (or coconut yoghurt for a dairy-free option) and unsweetened almond milk.
Add the flaxseeds and vanilla protein powder.
Add a handful of ice.
Blend on high until smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Coconut Cucumber Tonic
recipes.description: A light, refreshing and hydrating gut-healing smoothie perfect for after a cardio workout. Cucumber supports digestion and hydration, while coconut water delivers electrolytes, with mint and lime adding a zesty finish.

ingredients.name: cucumber
ingredients.raw_text: ½ cup cucumber, peeled and chopped

ingredients.name: unsweetened almond milk
ingredients.raw_text: ½ cup unsweetened almond milk (or any milk of choice)

ingredients.name: fresh mint leaves
ingredients.raw_text: Small handful of fresh mint leaves

ingredients.name: protein powder
ingredients.raw_text: 1 scoop unflavored or vanilla protein powder

steps: Peel and chop the cucumber into rough chunks.
Add the cucumber, frozen banana, coconut water, and almond milk to a blender.
Squeeze in the juice of half a lime.
Add the fresh mint leaves and protein powder.
Add a handful of ice.
Blend until smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Afterburn Elixir
recipes.description: A gut-healing smoothie harnessing the anti-inflammatory properties of turmeric and ginger with a fiber boost from banana. Supports immune health and is ideal pre-workout.

ingredients.name: fresh grated ginger
ingredients.raw_text: ½ tsp fresh grated ginger (or ground ginger)

ingredients.name: unsweetened oat milk
ingredients.raw_text: 1 cup unsweetened oat milk (or milk of choice)

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.raw_text: Pinch of black pepper (to enhance turmeric absorption)

steps: Add the frozen banana, ground turmeric, fresh grated ginger, and vanilla protein powder to a blender.
Pour in 1 cup of unsweetened oat milk.
Add flaxseed and a pinch of black pepper if using.
Add a handful of ice if desired.
Blend until smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Berry Blitz
recipes.description: A gut-health smoothie bursting with antioxidants and natural sweetness that supports digestion and helps reduce inflammation. Packed with mixed berries and a creamy base, it's a delicious way to nourish your gut and keep your energy steady.

ingredients.name: unsweetened almond milk
ingredients.raw_text: 1 cup unsweetened almond milk (or milk of choice)

steps: If using fresh bananas, freeze them ahead of time for best texture.
Add the mixed berries (strawberries, blueberries, raspberries) into a blender.
Add the frozen banana half to the blender.
Pour in the unsweetened almond milk.
Add protein powder and flaxseeds if using.
Add a handful of ice if desired.
Blend on high until smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Green Giant
recipes.description: A gut-healing green smoothie packed with nutrients and gentle to digest. Loaded with leafy greens, hydrating cucumber, and gut-friendly ingredients to keep your body balanced and calories low.
steps: Wash and prepare all fresh ingredients: rinse spinach, peel and chop cucumber, core and slice green apple.
Grate 1 teaspoon of fresh ginger.
Squeeze 1 tablespoon of fresh lemon juice.
Add spinach, cucumber, green apple, lemon juice, grated ginger, and coconut water (or water) into a blender.
Add a handful of ice if desired.
Blend on high until smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Matcha Muscle
recipes.description: A performance-boosting smoothie combining antioxidants from matcha, fiber from banana, and muscle-repairing protein powder to drive performance and elevate recovery after a resistance workout.

ingredients.name: protein powder
ingredients.raw_text: 1 scoop vanilla or unflavored protein powder

ingredients.name: unsweetened almond milk
ingredients.raw_text: 1 cup unsweetened almond milk (or any milk of choice)

ingredients.name: chia seeds
ingredients.raw_text: 1 tsp chia seeds (optional, for added fiber)

steps: Add the frozen banana to a blender.
Add the matcha powder and protein powder.
Pour in the unsweetened almond milk.
Add chia seeds if using, and a handful of ice if desired.
Blend on high until smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.
